title: Evaluating and Improving Automated Repository-Level Rust Issue Resolution with LLM-based Agents
authors: Jiahong Xiang, Wenxiao He, Xihua Wang, Hongliang Tian, Yuqun Zhang
abstract: The Rust programming language presents a steep learning curve and significant coding challenges, making the automation of issue resolution essential for its broader adoption. Recently, LLM-powered code agents have shown remarkable success in resolving complex software engineering tasks, yet their application to Rust has been limited by the absence of a large-scale, repository-level benchmark. To bridge this gap, we introduce Rust-SWE-bench, a benchmark comprising 500 real-world, repository-level software engineering tasks from 34 diverse and popular Rust repositories. We then perform a comprehensive study on Rust-SWE-bench with four representative agents and four state-of-the-art LLMs to establish a foundational understanding of their capabilities and limitations in the Rust ecosystem. Our extensive study reveals that while ReAct-style agents are promising, i.e., resolving up to 21.2% of issues, they are limited by two primary challenges: comprehending repository-wide code structure and complying with Rust's strict type and trait semantics. We also find that issue reproduction is rather critical for task resolution. Inspired by these findings, we propose RUSTFORGER, a novel agentic approach that integrates an automated test environment setup with a Rust metaprogramming-driven dynamic tracing strategy to facilitate reliable issue reproduction and dynamic analysis. The evaluation shows that RUSTFORGER using Claude-Sonnet-3.7 significantly outperforms all baselines, resolving 28.6% of tasks on Rust-SWE-bench, i.e., a 34.9% improvement over the strongest baseline, and, in aggregate, uniquely solves 46 tasks that no other agent could solve across all adopted advanced LLMs.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型代理的自动化仓库级Rust问题解决评估与优化
abstract_zh: Rust 编程语言具有陡峭的学习曲线和显著的编码挑战，因此自动化问题解决对于其更广泛的应用至关重要。近期，基于大语言模型（LLM）的代码智能体在解决复杂的软件工程任务方面表现出色，但其在 Rust 领域的应用仍受限于缺乏大规模、仓库级别的基准测试。为填补这一空白，我们提出了 Rust-SWE-bench，一个包含来自 34 个多样化且流行的 Rust 仓库的 500 个真实世界仓库级软件工程任务的基准测试集。随后，我们利用四种代表性智能体和四种最先进的 LLM 对 Rust-SWE-bench 进行了全面研究，以建立对这些智能体在 Rust 生态系统中能力与局限性的基础认知。我们的研究表明，尽管 ReAct 风格的智能体展现出一定潜力（最高可解决 21.2% 的问题），但其仍面临两大主要挑战：理解仓库级的代码结构，以及遵守 Rust 严格的类型和特质语义。此外，我们发现问题复现对于任务解决至关重要。基于这些发现，我们提出了 RUSTFORGER——一种新颖的智能体方法，该方法结合了自动测试环境搭建与基于 Rust 元编程的动态追踪策略，以实现可靠的问题复现与动态分析。评估结果表明，使用 Claude-Sonnet-3.7 的 RUSTFORGER 显著优于所有基线方法，在 Rust-SWE-bench 上成功解决了 28.6% 的任务，较最强基线提升 34.9%，并且总体上独立解决了 46 个其他所有先进 LLM 智能体均未能解决的任务。

title: Replacing Multi-Step Assembly of Data Preparation Pipelines with One-Step LLM Pipeline Generation for Table QA
authors: Fengyu Li, Junhao Zhu, Kaishi Song, Lu Chen, Zhongming Yao, Tianyi Li, Christian S. Jensen
abstract: Table Question Answering (TQA) aims to answer natural language questions over structured tables. Large Language Models (LLMs) enable promising solutions to this problem, with operator-centric solutions that generate table manipulation pipelines in a multi-step manner offering state-of-the-art performance. However, these solutions rely on multiple LLM calls, resulting in prohibitive latencies and computational costs. We propose Operation-R1, the first framework that trains lightweight LLMs (e.g., Qwen-4B/1.7B) via a novel variant of reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards to produce high-quality data-preparation pipelines for TQA in a single inference step. To train such an LLM, we first introduce a self-supervised rewarding mechanism to automatically obtain fine-grained pipeline-wise supervision signals for LLM training. We also propose variance-aware group resampling to mitigate training instability. To further enhance robustness of pipeline generation, we develop two complementary mechanisms: operation merge, which filters spurious operations through multi-candidate consensus, and adaptive rollback, which offers runtime protection against information loss in data transformation. Experiments on two benchmark datasets show that, with the same LLM backbone, Operation-R1 achieves average absolute accuracy gains of 9.55 and 6.08 percentage points over multi-step preparation baselines, with 79\% table compression and a 2.2$\times$ reduction in monetary cost.
title_zh: 用单步大语言模型管道生成替代多步骤数据准备流水线的组装用于表格问答
abstract_zh: 表格问答（Table Question Answering, TQA）旨在基于结构化表格回答自然语言问题。大型语言模型（LLMs）为该问题提供了极具前景的解决方案，其中以操作为中心的多步生成表格操作流水线的方法达到了当前最优性能。然而，这类方法依赖多次LLM调用，导致显著的延迟和高昂的计算成本。我们提出了Operation-R1，这是首个通过一种新型可验证奖励强化学习训练轻量级LLM（如Qwen-4B/1.7B）的框架，能够在单次推理步骤中生成高质量的数据预处理流水线，用于TQA任务。为训练此类LLM，我们首先引入一种自监督奖励机制，自动获取细粒度的流水线级监督信号，用于LLM训练；同时提出方差感知的分组重采样方法，以缓解训练过程中的不稳定性。为进一步提升流水线生成的鲁棒性，我们设计了两种互补机制：操作合并（operation merge），通过多候选共识过滤冗余操作；自适应回滚（adaptive rollback），在运行时提供保护，防止数据转换过程中的信息丢失。在两个基准数据集上的实验表明，使用相同的LLM骨干网络，Operation-R1相较于多步预处理基线方法，平均绝对准确率分别提升了9.55和6.08个百分点，同时实现79%的表格压缩率，并将经济成本降低2.2倍。

title: Systems-Level Attack Surface of Edge Agent Deployments on IoT
authors: Zhonghao Zhan, Krinos Li, Yefan Zhang, Hamed Haddadi
abstract: Edge deployment of LLM agents on IoT hardware introduces attack surfaces absent from cloud-hosted orchestration. We present an empirical security analysis of three architectures (cloud-hosted, edge-local swarm, and hybrid) using a multi-device home-automation testbed with local MQTT messaging and an Android smartphone as an edge inference node. We identify five systems-level attack surfaces, including two emergent failures observed during live testbed operation: coordination-state divergence and induced trust erosion. We frame core security properties as measurable systems metrics: data egress volume, failover window exposure, sovereignty boundary integrity, and provenance chain completeness. Our measurements show that edge-local deployments eliminate routine cloud data exposure but silently degrade sovereignty when fallback mechanisms trigger, with boundary crossings invisible at the application layer. Provenance chains remain complete under cooperative operation yet are trivially bypassed without cryptographic enforcement. Failover windows create transient blind spots exploitable for unauthorised actuation. These results demonstrate that deployment architecture, not just model or prompt design, is a primary determinant of security risk in agent-controlled IoT systems.
title_zh: 物联网中边缘代理部署的系统级攻击面
abstract_zh: 在物联网硬件上部署大型语言模型（LLM）代理引入了云端托管编排所不具备的攻击面。本文通过一个包含本地MQTT通信和Android智能手机作为边缘推理节点的多设备家庭自动化测试平台，对三种架构（云端托管、边缘本地群集和混合架构）进行了实证安全分析。我们识别出五个系统级攻击面，其中包括在实际测试平台运行过程中观察到的两种新型故障：协调状态分歧和诱导信任衰减。我们将核心安全属性定义为可量化的系统度量指标，包括数据外泄量、故障切换窗口暴露时间、主权边界完整性以及溯源链完整性。测量结果表明，边缘本地部署可消除常规的云端数据暴露，但在故障回退机制触发时会无声地削弱主权，且边界跨越在应用层完全不可见。在协作运行下，溯源链保持完整，但若缺乏密码学强制，其可被轻易绕过。故障切换窗口会形成短暂的盲区，可能被用于未经授权的设备操控。这些结果表明，在代理控制的物联网系统中，部署架构本身（而不仅仅是模型或提示设计）是决定安全风险的主要因素。

title: Cognitive Models and AI Algorithms Provide Templates for Designing Language Agents
authors: Ryan Liu, Dilip Arumugam, Cedegao E. Zhang, Sean Escola, Xaq Pitkow, Thomas L. Griffiths
abstract: While contemporary large language models (LLMs) are increasingly capable in isolation, there are still many difficult problems that lie beyond the abilities of a single LLM. For such tasks, there is still uncertainty about how best to take many LLMs as parts and combine them into a greater whole. This position paper argues that potential blueprints for designing such modular language agents can be found in the existing literature on cognitive models and artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms. To make this point clear, we formalize the idea of an agent template that specifies roles for individual LLMs and how their functionalities should be composed. We then survey a variety of existing language agents in the literature and highlight their underlying templates derived directly from cognitive models or AI algorithms. By highlighting these designs, we aim to call attention to agent templates inspired by cognitive science and AI as a powerful tool for developing effective, interpretable language agents.
title_zh: 认知模型与人工智能算法为设计语言代理提供了模板
abstract_zh: 尽管当代大型语言模型（LLMs）在独立运作时能力日益增强，但仍有许多复杂问题超出了单一LLM的能力范围。对于这类任务，如何将多个LLMs作为组件进行整合，形成更强大的整体系统，目前仍存在诸多不确定性。本文主张，设计此类模块化语言智能体的潜在蓝图，可以在现有的认知模型与人工智能（AI）算法文献中找到。为阐明这一观点，我们形式化提出一种“智能体模板”（agent template）的概念，用以明确各个LLM的角色及其功能组合方式。随后，我们综述了文献中多种现有的语言智能体，并指出它们所依赖的底层模板，这些模板直接源自认知模型或AI算法。通过揭示这些设计范式，我们旨在强调：受认知科学与人工智能启发的智能体模板，是一种开发高效、可解释语言智能体的强大工具。

title: STELLAR: Storage Tuning Engine Leveraging LLM Autonomous Reasoning for High Performance Parallel File Systems
authors: Chris Egersdoerfer, Philip Carns, Shane Snyder, Robert Ross, Dong Dai
abstract: I/O performance is crucial to efficiency in data-intensive scientific computing; but tuning large-scale storage systems is complex, costly, and notoriously manpower-intensive, making it inaccessible for most domain scientists. To address this problem, we propose STELLAR, an autonomous tuner for high-performance parallel file systems. Our evaluations show that STELLAR almost always selects near-optimal parameter configurations for parallel file systems within the first five attempts, even for previously unseen applications. STELLAR differs fundamentally from traditional autotuning methods, which often require hundreds of thousands of iterations to converge. Powered by large language models (LLMs), STELLAR enables autonomous end-to-end agentic tuning by (1) accurately extracting tunable parameters from software manuals, (2) analyzing I/O trace logs generated by applications, (3) selecting initial tuning strategies, (4) rerunning applications on real systems and collecting I/O performance feedback, (5) adjusting tuning strategies and repeating the tuning cycle, and (6) reflecting on and summarizing tuning experiences into reusable knowledge for future optimizations. STELLAR integrates retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), tool execution, LLM-based reasoning, and a multiagent design to stabilize reasoning and combat hallucinations. We evaluate the impact of each component on optimization outcomes, providing design insights for similar systems in other optimization domains. STELLAR's architecture and empirical results highlight a promising approach to complex system optimization, especially for problems with large search spaces and high exploration costs, while making I/O tuning more accessible to domain scientists with minimal added resources.
title_zh: STELLAR：基于大语言模型自主推理的存储调优引擎，用于高性能并行文件系统

title: Compress the Easy, Explore the Hard: Difficulty-Aware Entropy Regularization for Efficient LLM Reasoning
authors: Qin-Wen Luo, Sheng Ren, Xiang Chen, Rui Liu, Jun Fang, Naiqiang Tan, Sheng-Jun Huang
abstract: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) has substantially empowered Large Language Models (LLMs) to tackle complex reasoning tasks, yet the verbose nature of explicit reasoning steps incurs prohibitive inference latency and computational costs, limiting real-world deployment. While existing compression methods - ranging from self-training to Reinforcement Learning (RL) with length constraints - attempt to mitigate this, they often sacrifice reasoning capability for brevity. We identify a critical failure mode in these approaches: explicitly optimizing for shorter trajectories triggers rapid entropy collapse, which prematurely shrinks the exploration space and stifles the discovery of valid reasoning paths, particularly for challenging questions requiring extensive deduction. To address this issue, we propose Compress responses for Easy questions and Explore Hard ones (CEEH), a difficulty-aware approach to RL-based efficient reasoning. CEEH dynamically assesses instance difficulty to apply selective entropy regularization: it preserves a diverse search space for currently hard questions to ensure robustness, while permitting aggressive compression on easier instances where the reasoning path is well-established. In addition, we introduce a dynamic optimal-length penalty anchored to the historically shortest correct response, which effectively counteracts entropy-induced length inflation and stabilizes the reward signal. Across six reasoning benchmarks, CEEH consistently reduces response length while maintaining accuracy comparable to the base model, and improves Pass@k relative to length-only optimization.
title_zh: 轻松压缩，深入探索：面向高效大模型推理的难度感知熵正则化
abstract_zh: 思维链（Chain-of-Thought, CoT）显著提升了大型语言模型（LLMs）处理复杂推理任务的能力，然而显式推理步骤的冗长性导致了极高的推理延迟和计算开销，限制了其在现实场景中的部署。尽管现有的压缩方法——从自训练到带有长度约束的强化学习（RL）——试图缓解这一问题，但往往以牺牲推理能力为代价换取简洁性。我们发现这些方法存在一个关键缺陷：显式优化更短的推理轨迹会引发熵的快速坍缩，过早压缩探索空间，抑制了对有效推理路径的发现，尤其在需要大量推导的难题上表现尤为明显。为解决这一问题，我们提出一种难度感知的基于强化学习的高效推理方法——“对简单问题压缩响应，对难题探索路径”（Compress responses for Easy questions and Explore Hard ones, CEEH）。CEEH动态评估每个实例的难度，对不同问题实施选择性熵正则化：对当前较难的问题保留多样化的搜索空间，以确保推理鲁棒性；而对较简单的问题则允许进行激进压缩，因为其推理路径已较为明确。此外，我们引入一种基于历史最短正确响应的动态最优长度惩罚机制，有效抑制由熵引发的长度膨胀问题，并稳定奖励信号。在六个推理基准测试中，CEEH在保持与基线模型相当准确率的同时，持续减少响应长度，并在Pass@k指标上优于仅优化长度的方法。

title: TorchLean: Formalizing Neural Networks in Lean
authors: Robert Joseph George, Jennifer Cruden, Xiangru Zhong, Huan Zhang, Anima Anandkumar
abstract: Neural networks are increasingly deployed in safety- and mission-critical pipelines, yet many verification and analysis results are produced outside the programming environment that defines and runs the model. This separation creates a semantic gap between the executed network and the analyzed artifact, so guarantees can hinge on implicit conventions such as operator semantics, tensor layouts, preprocessing, and floating-point corner cases. We introduce TorchLean, a framework in the Lean 4 theorem prover that treats learned models as first-class mathematical objects with a single, precise semantics shared by execution and verification. TorchLean unifies (1) a PyTorch-style verified API with eager and compiled modes that lower to a shared op-tagged SSA/DAG computation-graph IR, (2) explicit Float32 semantics via an executable IEEE-754 binary32 kernel and proof-relevant rounding models, and (3) verification via IBP and CROWN/LiRPA-style bound propagation with certificate checking. We validate TorchLean end-to-end on certified robustness, physics-informed residual bounds for PINNs, and Lyapunov-style neural controller verification, alongside mechanized theoretical results including a universal approximation theorem. These results demonstrate a semantics-first infrastructure for fully formal, end-to-end verification of learning-enabled systems.
title_zh: TorchLean：在Lean中形式化神经网络
abstract_zh: 神经网络正越来越多地部署于安全关键和任务关键的流程中，然而，许多验证与分析结果却是在定义和运行模型的编程环境之外生成的。这种分离导致了执行的网络与分析对象之间的语义鸿沟，使得验证结果可能依赖于诸如算子语义、张量布局、预处理方式以及浮点数边缘情况等隐含约定。为此，我们提出了TorchLean，一个基于Lean 4定理证明器的框架，将学习得到的模型视为具有单一、精确语义的一等数学对象，该语义同时适用于模型的执行与验证。TorchLean实现了以下三个核心统一：（1）一个类似PyTorch的可验证API，支持急切执行与编译模式，二者均降级为共享的带操作标签的SSA/DAG计算图中间表示（IR）；（2）通过可执行的IEEE-754 binary32内核和具有证明相关性的舍入模型，显式定义Float32语义；（3）基于IBP以及CROWN/LiRPA风格的边界传播进行验证，并配备证书检查机制。我们在端到端层面验证了TorchLean在认证鲁棒性、物理信息残差边界（用于PINNs）、以及基于李雅普诺夫函数的神经控制器验证方面的有效性，同时结合了形式化理论成果，包括通用逼近定理的机械化证明。这些成果展示了以语义为先的基础设施，实现了学习型系统的完全形式化、端到端验证。

title: Requesting Expert Reasoning: Augmenting LLM Agents with Learned Collaborative Intervention
authors: Zhiming Wang, Jinwei He, Feng Lu
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM) based agents excel at general reasoning but often fail in specialized domains where success hinges on long-tail knowledge absent from their training data. While human experts can provide this missing knowledge, their guidance is often unstructured and unreliable, making its direct integration into an agent's plan problematic. To address this, we introduce AHCE (Active Human-Augmented Challenge Engagement), a framework for on-demand Human-AI collaboration. At its core, the Human Feedback Module (HFM) employs a learned policy to treat the human expert as an interactive reasoning tool. Extensive experiments in Minecraft demonstrate the framework's effectiveness, increasing task success rates by 32% on normal difficulty tasks and nearly 70% on highly difficult tasks, all with minimal human intervention. Our work demonstrates that successfully augmenting agents requires learning how to request expert reasoning, moving beyond simple requests for help.
title_zh: 请求专家推理：通过学习的协作干预增强大型语言模型代理
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的智能体在通用推理方面表现出色，但在需要长尾知识的特定领域中往往表现不佳，因为这些知识并未包含在其训练数据中。尽管人类专家可以提供这些缺失的知识，但他们的指导通常缺乏结构且不可靠，直接将其整合到智能体的决策计划中存在困难。为解决这一问题，我们提出了AHCE（主动人机增强式挑战参与）框架，实现按需的人机协作。该框架的核心是人类反馈模块（HFM），它采用学习得到的策略，将人类专家视为一个交互式的推理工具。在Minecraft中的大量实验表明，该框架具有显著效果：在普通难度任务上，任务成功率提升了32%；在高难度任务上，成功率更是接近提升了70%，且整个过程仅需极少的人类干预。我们的研究证明，成功增强智能体的关键在于学会如何请求专家的推理支持，而不仅仅是简单地寻求帮助。

title: RepoMod-Bench: A Benchmark for Code Repository Modernization via Implementation-Agnostic Testing
authors: Xuefeng Li, Nir Ben-Israel, Yotam Raz, Belal Ahmed, Doron Serebro, Antoine Raux
abstract: The evolution of AI coding agents has shifted the frontier from simple snippet completion to autonomous repository-level engineering. However, evaluating these agents remains ill-posed in general code repository generation, where the lack of deterministic ground truth leads to ambiguous metrics. Code modernization via automated translation offers a more rigorous alternative by providing a fixed ground truth -- the source repository; yet existing benchmarks are limited to small-scale repositories and rely on language-specific unit tests visible to the agent, allowing test-driven overfitting. We address these limitations by introducing a benchmarking framework for repository-level code modernization built on an implementation-agnostic evaluation paradigm. This framework is instantiated through RepoMod-Bench: a benchmark of 21 real-world repositories with standardized interfaces, spanning 8 programming languages. The benchmark contains 1.6M lines of code (LOC) and 11,616 tests, with repository sizes ranging from 14 to 211K LOC. By targeting repositories with standardized interfaces, we utilize an implementation-agnostic test suite to verify functional equivalence between source and target implementations. This black-box approach ensures verification remains consistent across languages, and our environment hides all test suites from agents to prevent test-driven shortcuts. Evaluating four state-of-the-art agent configurations reveals a sharp scaling collapse: average pass rates drop from 91.3% on projects under 10K LOC to 15.3% on projects exceeding 50K LOC. These results demonstrate that autonomous modernization at scale remains a significant open challenge. Our benchmark and code are available at https://github.com/Modelcode-ai/mcode-benchmark.
title_zh: RepoMod-Bench：一种通过与实现无关的测试进行代码仓库现代化的基准测试
abstract_zh: 人工智能编程代理的演进已将技术前沿从简单的代码片段补全，推进至自主的项目级工程。然而，在通用代码仓库生成任务中，由于缺乏确定性的真实答案（ground truth），对这些代理的评估仍存在根本性难题。通过自动化代码迁移实现的代码现代化提供了一种更为严谨的替代方案，因为它提供了固定的基准——即原始仓库；但现有基准测试仅限于小规模仓库，且依赖于代理可见的语言特定单元测试，导致测试驱动的过拟合问题。为解决上述局限，我们提出了一种基于与实现无关评估范式的仓库级代码现代化基准测试框架。该框架通过RepoMod-Bench得以具体实现：一个包含21个真实世界仓库的基准测试集，支持8种编程语言，具有标准化接口。该基准涵盖160万行代码（LOC）和11,616个测试用例，仓库规模从14K到211K LOC不等。通过聚焦具有标准化接口的仓库，我们采用与实现无关的测试套件，验证源代码与目标代码在功能上的等价性。这种黑盒方法确保了跨语言验证的一致性，同时我们的测试环境对代理完全隐藏所有测试套件，以防止其通过测试驱动的捷径。对四种前沿代理配置的评估显示，性能出现急剧下降：在代码量低于1万行的项目中，平均通过率高达91.3%，而在超过5万行的项目中，平均通过率骤降至15.3%。这些结果表明，大规模自主代码现代化仍是亟待解决的重大挑战。我们的基准测试数据集与代码已公开，访问地址为：https://github.com/Modelcode-ai/mcode-benchmark。

title: Why Diffusion Language Models Struggle with Truly Parallel (Non-Autoregressive) Decoding?
authors: Pengxiang Li, Dilxat Muhtar, Lu Yin, Tianlong Chen, Shiwei Liu
abstract: Diffusion Language Models (DLMs) are often advertised as enabling parallel token generation, yet practical fast DLMs frequently converge to left-to-right, autoregressive (AR)-like decoding dynamics. In contrast, genuinely non-AR generation is promising because it removes AR's sequential bottleneck, better exploiting parallel hardware to reduce synchronization/communication overhead and improve latency scaling with output length. We argue that a primary driver of AR-like decoding is a mismatch between DLM objectives and the highly sequential structure of widely used training data, including standard pretraining corpora and long chain-of-thought (CoT) supervision. Motivated by this diagnosis, we propose NAP (Non-Autoregressive Parallel DLMs), a proof-of-concept, data-centric approach that better aligns supervision with non-AR parallel decoding. NAP curates examples as multiple independent reasoning trajectories and couples them with a parallel-forced decoding strategy that encourages multi-token parallel updates. Across math reasoning benchmarks, NAP yields stronger performance under parallel decoding than DLMs trained on standard long CoT data, with gains growing as parallelism increases. Our results suggest that revisiting data and supervision is a principled direction for mitigating AR-like behavior and moving toward genuinely non-autoregressive parallel generation in DLMs. Our code is available at https://github.com/pixeli99/NAP.
title_zh: 为什么扩散语言模型在真正并行（非自回归）解码上会遇到困难？
abstract_zh: 扩散语言模型（DLMs）常被宣传为支持并行标记生成，然而在实际应用中，许多快速的DLM往往收敛到类似自回归（AR）的从左到右解码行为。相比之下，真正的非自回归（non-AR）生成具有巨大潜力，因为它消除了自回归方法的序列瓶颈，能够更充分地利用并行硬件，降低同步与通信开销，并改善输出长度增长时的延迟表现。我们认为，导致AR-like解码行为的一个主要原因是DLM的目标函数与广泛使用的训练数据高度序列化的结构之间存在不匹配，这些数据包括标准的预训练语料库以及长链式思维（CoT）监督数据。基于这一诊断，我们提出了NAP（Non-Autoregressive Parallel DLMs），一种以数据为中心的原型方法，旨在使监督信号更好地与非自回归并行解码对齐。NAP通过将训练样本组织为多个独立的推理轨迹，并结合一种强制并行解码策略，鼓励同时更新多个标记。在数学推理基准测试中，NAP在并行解码下的表现优于在标准长CoT数据上训练的传统DLM，且随着并行度的提升，性能优势愈发显著。我们的结果表明，重新审视训练数据与监督方式，是缓解AR-like行为、推动DLM实现真正非自回归并行生成的一条合理路径。相关代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/pixeli99/NAP。

title: LLM-Powered Silent Bug Fuzzing in Deep Learning Libraries via Versatile and Controlled Bug Transfer
authors: Kunpeng Zhang, Dongwei Xiao, Daoyuan Wu, Jiali Zhao, Yuanyi Lin, Tongtong Xu, Shaohua Wang, Shuai Wang
abstract: Deep learning (DL) libraries are widely used in critical applications, where even subtle silent bugs can lead to serious consequences. While existing DL fuzzing techniques have made progress in detecting crashes, they inherently struggle to detect silent bugs due to the lack of effective test programs and corresponding oracles. Building on the observation that historical bug reports contain rich, underutilized information about silent bugs, we leverage large language models (LLMs) to perform versatile yet controlled bug transfer for silent bug fuzzing. Specifically, our approach uses LLMs to extract context-aware bug patterns from historical issues, match semantically related Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) using functionality-based embeddings, and synthesize test cases with customized oracles. This enables proactive detection of silent bugs by transferring high-risk contexts and oracle designs from known buggy APIs to functionally similar target APIs. To ensure the reliability of our context-aware bug transfer, we introduce an LLM-powered self-validation module that systematically evaluates the validity of each transferred bug instance. We implement this methodology in a tool named TransFuzz and evaluate it on three mainstream DL libraries: PyTorch, TensorFlow, and MindSpore. TransFuzz successfully discovers 79 previously unknown bugs (12 confirmed as Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVEs)) in 10 bug types, demonstrating its effectiveness and generalizability in migrating DL library bug discovery capabilities.
title_zh: 基于大语言模型的深度学习库中通用且可控的漏洞转移静默漏洞模糊测试
abstract_zh: 深度学习（DL）库被广泛应用于关键任务场景中，即使微小的隐性错误也可能导致严重后果。尽管现有的深度学习模糊测试技术在检测崩溃方面已取得一定进展，但由于缺乏有效的测试程序和相应的判定标准（oracle），它们在发现隐性错误方面存在固有局限。基于一个观察：历史错误报告中蕴含着丰富但尚未被充分利用的隐性错误信息，我们利用大规模语言模型（LLM）实现灵活而可控的隐性错误迁移，以提升隐性错误的模糊测试能力。具体而言，我们的方法通过LLM从历史问题中提取上下文感知的错误模式，利用基于功能的嵌入技术匹配语义相关的应用程序编程接口（API），并生成带有定制化判定标准的测试用例。这一机制能够通过将已知存在缺陷的API中的高风险上下文和判定标准设计，迁移至功能相似的目标API，从而实现对隐性错误的主动检测。为确保这种上下文感知错误迁移的可靠性，我们引入了一个由LLM驱动的自我验证模块，系统性地评估每个迁移后错误实例的有效性。我们将该方法实现为名为TransFuzz的工具，并在三个主流深度学习库——PyTorch、TensorFlow和MindSpore上进行了评估。TransFuzz成功发现了79个此前未知的缺陷（其中12个已被确认为通用漏洞披露（CVE）），覆盖10种缺陷类型，充分证明了该方法在迁移深度学习库缺陷发现能力方面的有效性与通用性。

title: Mirroring the Mind: Distilling Human-Like Metacognitive Strategies into Large Language Models
authors: Ik-hwan Kim, Hyeongrok Han, Mingi Jung, Sangwon Yu, Jinseok Hong, Sang Hun Kim, Yoonyoung Choi, Sungroh Yoon
abstract: Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) often exhibit structural fragility in complex reasoning tasks, failing to produce correct answers even after successfully deriving valid intermediate steps. Through systematic analysis, we observe that these failures frequently stem not from a lack of reasoning capacity, but from a deficiency in self-regulatory control, where valid logic is destabilized by uncontrolled exploration or the failure to recognize logical sufficiency. Motivated by this observation, we propose Metacognitive Behavioral Tuning (MBT), a post-training framework that explicitly injects metacognitive behaviors into the model's thought process. MBT implements this via two complementary formulations: (1) MBT-S, which synthesizes rigorous reasoning traces from scratch, and (2) MBT-R, which rewrites the student's initial traces to stabilize intrinsic exploration patterns. Experiments across multi-hop QA benchmarks demonstrate that MBT consistently outperforms baselines, achieving notable gains on challenging benchmarks. By effectively eliminating reasoning collapse, MBT achieves higher accuracy with significantly reduced token consumption, demonstrating that internalizing metacognitive strategies leads to more stable and robust reasoning.
title_zh: 镜像思维：将类人的元认知策略提炼至大型语言模型
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）在复杂推理任务中常表现出结构上的脆弱性，即使成功推导出有效的中间步骤，仍可能无法得出正确答案。通过系统性分析，我们发现这些失败往往并非源于推理能力的不足，而是由于缺乏自我调控能力：有效的逻辑推理过程被不受控制的探索行为所干扰，或未能识别出逻辑上的充分性。基于这一观察，我们提出了元认知行为调优（Metacognitive Behavioral Tuning, MBT），一种后训练框架，旨在显式地将元认知行为注入模型的思考过程。MBT通过两种互补的实现方式来达成目标：（1）MBT-S，从零开始生成严谨的推理轨迹；（2）MBT-R，对学生的初始推理轨迹进行重写，以稳定其内在的探索模式。在多跳问答基准测试中的实验表明，MBT始终优于各类基线方法，在具有挑战性的基准上取得了显著提升。通过有效防止推理崩溃，MBT在大幅降低token消耗的同时实现了更高的准确率，证明了内化元认知策略能够带来更稳定、更鲁棒的推理能力。

title: static_maps: consteval std::map and std::unordered_map Implementations in C++23
authors: Isaac D. Myhal, Oliver Serang
abstract: Using consteval from C++23, we implement efficient, new versions of std::map and std::unordered_map for use when the keys are known at compile time. We demonstrate superior performance of our unordered_map on three demonstration use-cases: Lookup of elemental mass from atomic symbol, lookup of amino acid from codon, and modification of stock prices from S&P 500 ticker symbols all produced runtimes <40%, <35%, <73% of the respective runtimes of the std implementations. Our library runimes were <80%, <45%, <97% of the lookup time of Frozen, an alternative perfect hashing implementation in C++ for problems also using constexpr keys. To our knowledge, this makes our library the overall fastest drop-in (i.e., with a similar API) alternative to std::unordered_map. On one arbitrarily chosen demo, we demonstrate runtimes <35% of PTHash and <89% gperf, state-of-the-art but not drop-in hashing libraries via external tools.
title_zh: 静态地图：C++23 中的 consteval std::map 和 std::unordered_map 实现
abstract_zh: 利用 C++23 的 `consteval`，我们实现了高效的 `std::map` 和 `std::unordered_map` 的新版本，适用于键在编译时已知的场景。我们在三个演示用例中展示了我们实现的 `unordered_map` 的卓越性能：根据原子符号查找元素质量、根据密码子查找氨基酸，以及根据标普500股票代码修改股价。在这些场景中，我们的实现运行时间分别低于标准库实现的 40%、35% 和 73%。与另一种使用 `constexpr` 键的 C++ 完美哈希实现 Frozen 相比，我们的库运行时间分别低于其查找时间的 80%、45% 和 97%。据我们所知，这使得我们的库成为目前性能最优的、可直接替换 `std::unordered_map` 的替代方案（即具有相似 API 的替代品）。在其中一个任意选择的演示案例中，我们进一步展示了运行时间低于 PTHash 的 35% 以及低于 gperf 的 89%，而 PTHash 和 gperf 是当前最先进的哈希库，但它们并非直接可替换的库，需通过外部工具集成。

title: Exploratory Memory-Augmented LLM Agent via Hybrid On- and Off-Policy Optimization
authors: Zeyuan Liu, Jeonghye Kim, Xufang Luo, Dongsheng Li, Yuqing Yang
abstract: Exploration remains the key bottleneck for large language model agents trained with reinforcement learning. While prior methods exploit pretrained knowledge, they fail in environments requiring the discovery of novel states. We propose Exploratory Memory-Augmented On- and Off-Policy Optimization (EMPO$^2$), a hybrid RL framework that leverages memory for exploration and combines on- and off-policy updates to make LLMs perform well with memory while also ensuring robustness without it. On ScienceWorld and WebShop, EMPO$^2$ achieves 128.6% and 11.3% improvements over GRPO, respectively. Moreover, in out-of-distribution tests, EMPO$^2$ demonstrates superior adaptability to new tasks, requiring only a few trials with memory and no parameter updates. These results highlight EMPO$^2$ as a promising framework for building more exploratory and generalizable LLM-based agents.
title_zh: 基于混合在线与离线策略优化的探索性记忆增强型大语言模型代理
abstract_zh: 探索仍是通过强化学习训练的大语言模型智能体面临的关键瓶颈。尽管先前的方法利用了预训练知识，但在需要发现新状态的环境中表现不佳。我们提出了探索性记忆增强的在线与离线策略优化方法（EMPO$^2$），这是一种混合强化学习框架，通过记忆机制促进探索，并结合在线与离线策略更新，使大语言模型在具备记忆能力时表现优异，同时在无记忆情况下仍保持鲁棒性。在ScienceWorld和WebShop任务中，EMPO$^2$分别相较于GRPO实现了128.6%和11.3%的性能提升。此外，在分布外测试中，EMPO$^2$展现出卓越的适应新任务的能力，仅需少量试错即可完成学习，且无需参数更新。这些结果表明，EMPO$^2$是一个极具前景的框架，有助于构建更具探索性和泛化能力的大语言模型智能体。

title: Accelerating Local LLMs on Resource-Constrained Edge Devices via Distributed Prompt Caching
authors: Hiroki Matsutani, Naoki Matsuda, Naoto Sugiura
abstract: Since local LLM inference on resource-constrained edge devices imposes a severe performance bottleneck, this paper proposes distributed prompt caching to enhance inference performance by cooperatively sharing intermediate processing states across multiple low-end edge devices. To fully utilize prompt similarity, our distributed caching mechanism also supports partial matching. As this approach introduces communication overhead associated with state sharing over a wireless network, we introduce a Bloom-filter-based data structure, referred to as a catalog, to determine whether a remote server possesses the desired internal states, thereby suppressing unnecessary communication. Experiments using the Gemma-3 270M model and the MMLU dataset on the Raspberry Pi Zero 2W platform demonstrate that the proposed approach reduces TTFT (Time to First Token) and TTLT (Time to Last Token) by 93.12% and 50.07% on average, respectively.
title_zh: 通过分布式提示缓存加速资源受限边缘设备上的本地大模型
abstract_zh: 由于在资源受限的边缘设备上进行本地大语言模型（LLM）推理会带来严重的性能瓶颈，本文提出了一种分布式提示缓存机制，通过多个低端边缘设备协同共享中间处理状态，以提升推理性能。为充分挖掘提示之间的相似性，我们的分布式缓存机制还支持部分匹配。由于该方法涉及通过无线网络共享状态，带来了通信开销，因此我们引入了一种基于布隆过滤器的数据结构，称为“目录（catalog）”，用于判断远程服务器是否拥有所需的内部状态，从而有效抑制不必要的通信。在Raspberry Pi Zero 2W平台上，基于Gemma-3 270M模型和MMLU数据集的实验表明，所提出的方法平均将TTFT（首个标记生成时间）和TTLT（最后一个标记生成时间）分别降低了93.12%和50.07%。

title: ParamMem: Augmenting Language Agents with Parametric Reflective Memory
authors: Tianjun Yao, Yongqiang Chen, Yujia Zheng, Pan Li, Zhiqiang Shen, Kun Zhang
abstract: Self-reflection enables language agents to iteratively refine solutions, yet often produces repetitive outputs that limit reasoning performance. Recent studies have attempted to address this limitation through various approaches, among which increasing reflective diversity has shown promise. Our empirical analysis reveals a strong positive correlation between reflective diversity and task success, further motivating the need for diverse reflection signals. We introduce ParamMem, a parametric memory module that encodes cross-sample reflection patterns into model parameters, enabling diverse reflection generation through temperature-controlled sampling. Building on this module, we propose ParamAgent, a reflection-based agent framework that integrates parametric memory with episodic and cross-sample memory. Extensive experiments on code generation, mathematical reasoning, and multi-hop question answering demonstrate consistent improvements over state-of-the-art baselines. Further analysis reveals that ParamMem is sample-efficient, enables weak-to-strong transfer across model scales, and supports self-improvement without reliance on stronger external model, highlighting the potential of ParamMem as an effective component for enhancing language agents.
title_zh: ParamMem：通过参数化反思记忆增强语言代理
abstract_zh: 自我反思使语言代理能够迭代优化解决方案，但常常导致重复性输出，从而限制了推理性能。近期研究尝试通过多种方法解决这一局限，其中增加反思的多样性展现出良好前景。我们的实证分析揭示了反思多样性与任务成功率之间存在显著正相关关系，进一步凸显了多样化反思信号的重要性。为此，我们提出了ParamMem——一种参数化记忆模块，能够将跨样本的反思模式编码到模型参数中，通过温度控制采样实现多样化的反思生成。基于该模块，我们构建了ParamAgent——一种融合参数化记忆、情景记忆与跨样本记忆的基于反思的代理框架。在代码生成、数学推理及多跳问答任务上的大量实验表明，该框架在性能上持续优于当前最先进的基线方法。进一步分析显示，ParamMem具有良好的样本效率，能够在不同模型规模间实现从弱到强的迁移能力，并支持无需依赖更强外部模型的自我改进，凸显了ParamMem作为提升语言代理效能的有效组件的巨大潜力。

title: Mitigating Legibility Tax with Decoupled Prover-Verifier Games
authors: Yegon Kim, Juho Lee
abstract: As large language models become increasingly capable, it is critical that their outputs can be easily checked by less capable systems. Prover-verifier games can be used to improve checkability of model outputs, but display a degradation in accuracy compared to a baseline trained only to maximize correctness -- a phenonemon named legibility tax. We propose a solution by decoupling the correctness from the checkability condition and instead training a "translator" model that turns a fixed solver model's solution into a checkable form. This allows us to first train the solver to maximize correctness, and then train the translator to translate the solver into a checkable form while retaining the solver's answer. To accommodate this new objective of translation, we formulate a decoupled prover-verifier game where the equilibria correspond to faithful and checkable translators.
title_zh: 通过解耦证明者-验证者博弈缓解可读性税
abstract_zh: 随着大型语言模型能力的不断提升，确保其输出结果能够被能力较弱的系统轻松验证变得至关重要。证明者-验证者博弈可用于提升模型输出的可验证性，但相较于仅以最大化正确性为目标训练的基线模型，其准确率会出现下降——这一现象被称为“可读性代价”。为此，我们提出一种解决方案：将正确性与可验证性条件解耦，转而训练一个“翻译器”模型，将固定求解器模型的解答转换为可验证的形式。这种方法使我们能够先训练求解器以最大化正确性，再训练翻译器将求解器的输出转化为可验证格式，同时保留求解器原有的答案。为适应这一新的翻译目标，我们提出了一个解耦的证明者-验证者博弈框架，其均衡状态对应于忠实且可验证的翻译器。

title: Array-Carrying Symbolic Execution for Function Contract Generation
authors: Weijie Lu, Jingyu Ke, Hongfei Fu, Zhouyue Sun, Yi Zhou, Guoqiang Li, Haokun Li
abstract: Function contract generation is a classical problem in program analysis that targets the automated analysis of functions in a program with multiple procedures. The problem is fundamental in inter-procedural analysis where properties of functions are first obtained via the generation of function contracts and then the generated contracts are used as building blocks to analyze the whole program. Typical objectives in function contract generation include pre-/post-conditions and assigns information (that specifies the modification information over program variables and memory segments during function execution). In programs with array manipulations, a crucial point in function contract generation is the treatment of array segments that imposes challenges in inferring invariants and assigns information over such segments. To address this challenge, we propose a novel symbolic execution framework that carries invariants and assigns information over contiguous segments of arrays. We implement our framework as a prototype within LLVM, and further integrate our prototype with the ACSL assertion format and the Frama-C software verification platform. Experimental evaluation over a variety of benchmarks from the literature and functions from realistic libraries shows that our framework is capable of handling array manipulating functions that indeed involve the carry of array information and are beyond existing approaches.
title_zh: 数组携带的符号执行在函数契约生成中的应用
abstract_zh: 函数契约生成是程序分析中的一个经典问题，旨在对具有多个过程的程序中的函数进行自动化分析。该问题在过程间分析中具有基础性意义：首先通过生成函数契约获取函数的性质，然后利用生成的契约作为构建模块来分析整个程序。函数契约生成的典型目标包括前置条件、后置条件以及 assigns 信息（用于指定函数执行过程中程序变量和内存段的修改情况）。在涉及数组操作的程序中，函数契约生成的一个关键难点在于对数组段的处理，这给推断这些段上的不变量和 assigns 信息带来了挑战。为应对这一挑战，我们提出了一种新颖的符号执行框架，能够携带连续数组段上的不变量和 assigns 信息。我们已将该框架实现为 LLVM 内的一个原型，并进一步将其与 ACSL 断言格式及 Frama-C 软件验证平台集成。在文献中的多种基准测试以及真实库函数上的实验评估表明，我们的框架能够有效处理涉及数组信息传递的函数，这些函数超出了现有方法的处理能力。

title: General Agent Evaluation
authors: Elron Bandel, Asaf Yehudai, Lilach Eden, Yehoshua Sagron, Yotam Perlitz, Elad Venezian, Natalia Razinkov, Natan Ergas, Shlomit Shachor Ifergan, Segev Shlomov, Michal Jacovi, Leshem Choshen, Liat Ein-Dor, Yoav Katz, Michal Shmueli-Scheuer
abstract: The promise of general-purpose agents - systems that perform tasks in unfamiliar environments without domain-specific engineering - remains largely unrealized. Existing agents are predominantly specialized, and while emerging implementations like OpenAI SDK Agent and Claude Code hint at broader capabilities, no systematic evaluation of their general performance has been pursued. Current agentic benchmarks assume domain-specific integration, encoding task information in ways that preclude fair evaluation of general agents. This paper frames general-agent evaluation as a first-class research objective. We propose conceptual principles for such evaluation, a Unified Protocol enabling agent-benchmark integration, and Exgentic - a practical framework for general agent evaluation. We benchmark five prominent agent implementations across six environments as the first Open General Agent Leaderboard. Our experiments show that general agents generalize across diverse environments, achieving performance comparable to domain-specific agents without any environment-specific tuning. We release our evaluation protocol, framework, and leaderboard to establish a foundation for systematic research on general-purpose agents.
title_zh: 通用代理评估
abstract_zh: 通用代理的承诺——即在陌生环境中无需领域特定工程即可执行任务的系统——至今仍 largely未能实现。现有的代理系统大多具有高度专业化特性，尽管像 OpenAI SDK Agent 和 Claude Code 等新兴实现已展现出更广泛的能力迹象，但尚未有系统性评估来全面衡量其通用性能。当前的代理评估基准普遍假设了领域特定的集成，将任务信息以预设方式编码，从而无法对通用代理进行公平评估。本文将通用代理评估提升为一项核心研究目标。我们提出了通用评估的概念性原则，设计了一种统一协议（Unified Protocol）以实现代理与基准的无缝集成，并开发了 Exgentic——一个面向通用代理评估的实用框架。我们基于六个不同环境，对五种主流代理实现进行了基准测试，首次推出了“开放通用代理排行榜”（Open General Agent Leaderboard）。实验结果表明，通用代理能够在多种异构环境中实现良好泛化，其性能可与领域特定代理相媲美，且无需任何针对特定环境的调优。我们已公开发布评估协议、框架及排行榜，旨在为通用代理的系统性研究奠定坚实基础。

title: Test-Time Scaling with Diffusion Language Models via Reward-Guided Stitching
authors: Roy Miles, Aysim Toker, Andreea-Maria Oncescu, Songcen Xu, Jiankang Deng, Ismail Elezi
abstract: Reasoning with large language models often benefits from generating multiple chains-of-thought, but existing aggregation strategies are typically trajectory-level (e.g., selecting the best trace or voting on the final answer), discarding useful intermediate work from partial or "nearly correct" attempts. We propose Stitching Noisy Diffusion Thoughts, a self-consistency framework that turns cheap diffusion-sampled reasoning into a reusable pool of step-level candidates. Given a problem, we (i) sample many diverse, low-cost reasoning trajectories using a masked diffusion language model, (ii) score every intermediate step with an off-the-shelf process reward model (PRM), and (iii) stitch these highest-quality steps across trajectories into a composite rationale. This rationale then conditions an autoregressive (AR) model (solver) to recompute only the final answer. This modular pipeline separates exploration (diffusion) from evaluation and solution synthesis, avoiding monolithic unified hybrids while preserving broad search. Across math reasoning benchmarks, we find that step-level recombination is most beneficial on harder problems, and ablations highlight the importance of the final AR solver in converting stitched but imperfect rationales into accurate answers. Using low-confidence diffusion sampling with parallel, independent rollouts, our training-free framework improves average accuracy by up to 23.8% across six math and coding tasks. At the same time, it achieves up to a 1.8x latency reduction relative to both traditional diffusion models (e.g., Dream, LLaDA) and unified architectures (e.g., TiDAR). Code is available at https://github.com/roymiles/diffusion-stitching.
title_zh: 通过奖励引导拼接实现扩散语言模型的测试时缩放
abstract_zh:
使用大型语言模型进行推理时，生成多个思维链（chains-of-thought）通常能带来显著收益，但现有的聚合策略多为轨迹级（例如选择最佳推理路径或对最终答案进行投票），往往忽略了部分或“几乎正确”尝试中蕴含的有用中间步骤。为此，我们提出了**Stitching Noisy Diffusion Thoughts**（噪声扩散思维缝合）——一种自一致性框架，将低成本采样的扩散式推理转化为可复用的步骤级候选池。

给定一个待解决的问题，我们的方法包含三个步骤：  
(i) 利用掩码扩散语言模型（masked diffusion language model）采样大量多样化、低成本的推理轨迹；  
(ii) 使用现成的流程奖励模型（Process Reward Model, PRM）对每个中间步骤进行评分；  
(iii) 将来自不同轨迹中质量最高的步骤进行拼接，形成一个综合性的推理链条。

随后，该合成的推理链作为条件输入，驱动一个自回归（AR）模型（即求解器）仅重新计算最终答案。这一模块化流程将探索（扩散采样）与评估、解法合成分离，避免了复杂统一模型的冗余，同时保留了广泛的搜索能力。

在多个数学推理基准测试中，我们发现：**步骤级重组在更难的问题上收益最为显著**；消融实验也表明，最终的自回归求解器在将拼接而成但不完美的推理链转化为准确答案方面至关重要。

通过采用低置信度的扩散采样方式，并行独立地展开多个推理路径，我们的训练-free框架在六项数学与编程任务上，平均准确率最高提升了 **23.8%**。与此同时，相比传统扩散模型（如 Dream、LLaDA）和统一架构（如 TiDAR），该方法在推理延迟上最高可降低 **1.8倍**。

代码已开源，地址为：https://github.com/roymiles/diffusion-stitching。

title: MiroFlow: Towards High-Performance and Robust Open-Source Agent Framework for General Deep Research Tasks
authors: Shiqian Su, Sen Xing, Xuan Dong, Muyan Zhong, Bin Wang, Xizhou Zhu, Yuntao Chen, Wenhai Wang, Yue Deng, Pengxiang Zhu, Ziyuan Liu, Tiantong Li, Jiaheng Yu, Zhe Chen, Lidong Bing, Jifeng Dai
abstract: Despite the remarkable progress of large language models (LLMs), the capabilities of standalone LLMs have begun to plateau when tackling real-world, complex tasks that require interaction with external tools and dynamic environments. Although recent agent frameworks aim to enhance model autonomy through tool integration and external interaction, they still suffer from naive workflows, unstable performance, limited support across diverse benchmarks and tasks, and heavy reliance on costly commercial APIs. In this work, we propose a high-performance and robust open-source agent framework, termed MiroFlow, which incorporates an agent graph for flexible orchestration, an optional deep reasoning mode to enhance performance, and a robust workflow execution to ensure stable and reproducible performance. Extensive experiments demonstrate that MiroFlow consistently achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple agent benchmarks, including GAIA, BrowseComp-EN/ZH, HLE, xBench-DeepSearch, and notably FutureX. We hope it could serve as an easily accessible, reproducible, and comparable baseline for the deep research community.
title_zh: MiroFlow：面向通用深度研究任务的高性能且稳健的开源智能体框架
abstract_zh: 尽管大型语言模型（LLMs）取得了显著进展，但在处理需要与外部工具及动态环境交互的现实世界复杂任务时，独立运行的LLM能力已开始趋于瓶颈。尽管近期的智能体框架通过工具集成和外部交互试图提升模型的自主性，但仍存在流程设计简单、性能不稳定、跨多样化基准和任务的支持有限，以及对昂贵商业API高度依赖等问题。本文提出了一种高性能且稳健的开源智能体框架——MiroFlow，其核心包括：用于灵活编排的智能体图结构、可选的深度推理模式以提升性能，以及稳健的工作流执行机制，以确保稳定且可复现的表现。大量实验表明，MiroFlow在多个智能体基准测试中持续达到最先进水平，涵盖GAIA、BrowseComp-EN/ZH、HLE、xBench-DeepSearch，以及尤为重要的FutureX。我们希望该框架能为深度研究社区提供一个易于获取、可复现且可比较的基准。

title: AMA-Bench: Evaluating Long-Horizon Memory for Agentic Applications
authors: Yujie Zhao, Boqin Yuan, Junbo Huang, Haocheng Yuan, Zhongming Yu, Haozhou Xu, Lanxiang Hu, Abhilash Shankarampeta, Zimeng Huang, Wentao Ni, Yuandong Tian, Jishen Zhao
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are deployed as autonomous agents in increasingly complex applications, where enabling long-horizon memory is critical for achieving strong performance. However, a significant gap exists between practical applications and current evaluation standards for agent memory: existing benchmarks primarily focus on dialogue-centric, human-agent interactions. In reality, agent memory consists of a continuous stream of agent-environment interactions that are primarily composed of machine-generated representations. To bridge this gap, we introduce AMA-Bench (Agent Memory with Any length), which evaluates long-horizon memory for LLMs in real agentic applications. It features two key components: (1) a set of real-world agentic trajectories across representative agentic applications, paired with expert-curated QA, and (2) a set of synthetic agentic trajectories that scale to arbitrary horizons, paired with rule-based QA. Our comprehensive study shows that existing memory systems underperform on AMA-Bench primarily because they lack causality and objective information and are constrained by the lossy nature of similarity-based retrieval employed by many memory systems. To address these limitations, we propose AMA-Agent, an effective memory system featuring a causality graph and tool-augmented retrieval. Our results demonstrate that AMA-Agent achieves 57.22% average accuracy on AMA-Bench, surpassing the strongest memory system baselines by 11.16%.
title_zh: AMA-Bench：评估智能体应用中的长时记忆能力
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正被部署为自主智能体，应用于日益复杂的场景中，而实现长时程记忆对于取得优异性能至关重要。然而，当前实际应用与现有智能体记忆评估标准之间存在显著差距：现有基准测试主要聚焦于以对话为中心的人机交互场景。实际上，智能体记忆是由持续不断的智能体-环境交互构成的，这些交互主要由机器生成的表征组成。为弥合这一差距，我们提出了AMA-Bench（任意长度的智能体记忆评估基准），用于在真实智能体应用中评估LLM的长时程记忆能力。该基准包含两个核心组成部分：（1）一系列涵盖代表性智能体应用的真实世界智能体轨迹，配以专家精心标注的问答对；（2）一组可扩展至任意时长的合成智能体轨迹，配以基于规则的问答对。我们的全面研究表明，现有记忆系统在AMA-Bench上表现不佳，主要原因在于它们缺乏因果关系建模和客观信息表达，并且受限于许多记忆系统所采用的基于相似性的检索方式所带来的信息损失。为解决这些局限，我们提出了AMA-Agent，一种高效的记忆系统，其核心特征包括因果图结构和工具增强的检索机制。实验结果表明，AMA-Agent在AMA-Bench上实现了57.22%的平均准确率，相比最强的基线记忆系统提升了11.16%。

title: DPSQL+: A Differentially Private SQL Library with a Minimum Frequency Rule
authors: Tomoya Matsumoto, Shokichi Takakura, Shun Takagi, Satoshi Hasegawa
abstract: SQL is the de facto interface for exploratory data analysis; however, releasing exact query results can expose sensitive information through membership or attribute inference attacks. Differential privacy (DP) provides rigorous privacy guarantees, but in practice, DP alone may not satisfy governance requirements such as the \emph{minimum frequency rule}, which requires each released group (cell) to include contributions from at least $k$ distinct individuals. In this paper, we present \textbf{DPSQL+}, a privacy-preserving SQL library that simultaneously enforces user-level $(\varepsilon,δ)$-DP and the minimum frequency rule. DPSQL+ adopts a modular architecture consisting of: (i) a \emph{Validator} that statically restricts queries to a DP-safe subset of SQL; (ii) an \emph{Accountant} that consistently tracks cumulative privacy loss across multiple queries; and (iii) a \emph{Backend} that interfaces with various database engines, ensuring portability and extensibility. Experiments on the TPC-H benchmark demonstrate that DPSQL+ achieves practical accuracy across a wide range of analytical workloads -- from basic aggregates to quadratic statistics and join operations -- and allows substantially more queries under a fixed global privacy budget than prior libraries in our evaluation.
title_zh: DPSQL+：一种基于最小频率规则的差分隐私SQL库
abstract_zh: SQL 是探索性数据分析的事实标准接口；然而，直接发布精确的查询结果可能通过成员身份推断或属性推断攻击暴露敏感信息。差分隐私（DP）提供了严格的隐私保障，但在实际应用中，仅依赖差分隐私可能无法满足诸如“最小频率规则”等治理要求，该规则要求每个发布的数据组（单元格）必须包含至少 $k$ 个不同个体的贡献。本文提出 **DPSQL+**，一个隐私保护的 SQL 库，能够同时满足用户级别的 $(\varepsilon, δ)$-差分隐私和最小频率规则。DPSQL+ 采用模块化架构，包含：(i) 一个**验证器**（Validator），静态地将查询限制在安全的差分隐私子集 SQL 范围内；(ii) 一个**会计模块**（Accountant），持续跟踪多次查询累积的隐私损失；(iii) 一个**后端**（Backend），与多种数据库引擎对接，确保系统的可移植性和可扩展性。在 TPC-H 基准测试上的实验表明，DPSQL+ 在广泛的分析工作负载中（从基础聚合到二次统计量及连接操作）均实现了良好的实用性与高精度，并在固定全局隐私预算下支持的查询数量显著多于我们评估中其他现有库。

title: EvolveGen: Algorithmic Level Hardware Model Checking Benchmark Generation through Reinforcement Learning
authors: Guangyu Hu, Xiaofeng Zhou, Wei Zhang, Hongce Zhang
abstract: Progress in hardware model checking depends critically on high-quality benchmarks. However, the community faces a significant benchmark gap: existing suites are limited in number, often distributed only in representations such as BTOR2 without access to the originating register-transfer-level (RTL) designs, and biased toward extreme difficulty where instances are either trivial or intractable. These limitations hinder rigorous evaluation of new verification techniques and encourage overfitting of solver heuristics to a narrow set of problems. To address this, we introduce EvolveGen, a framework for generating hardware model checking benchmarks by combining reinforcement learning (RL) with high-level synthesis (HLS). Our approach operates at an algorithmic level of abstraction in which an RL agent learns to construct computation graphs. By compiling these graphs under different synthesis directives, we produce pairs of functionally equivalent but structurally distinct hardware designs, inducing challenging model checking instances. Solver runtime is used as the reward signal, enabling the agent to autonomously discover and generate small-but-hard instances that expose solver-specific weaknesses. Experiments show that EvolveGen efficiently creates a diverse benchmark set in standard formats (e.g., AIGER and BTOR2) and effectively reveals performance bottlenecks in state-of-the-art model checkers.
title_zh: EvolveGen：通过强化学习实现算法级硬件模型检测基准生成
abstract_zh: 硬件模型检查技术的进步在很大程度上依赖于高质量的基准测试。然而，当前社区面临显著的基准缺口：现有的基准测试集数量有限，通常仅以BTOR2等格式提供，缺乏原始的寄存器传输级（RTL）设计来源，且偏向于极端难度——即测试实例要么过于简单，要么完全无法求解。这些局限性阻碍了对新型验证技术的严格评估，也导致求解器启发式策略过度拟合于少数特定问题。为解决这一问题，我们提出了EvolveGen，一种结合强化学习（RL）与高层次综合（HLS）的硬件模型检查基准生成框架。我们的方法在算法级抽象层面运行，其中强化学习代理学习构建计算图。通过在不同综合指令下编译这些计算图，我们生成功能等价但结构迥异的硬件设计对，从而构造出具有挑战性的模型检查实例。以求解器运行时间作为奖励信号，该代理能够自主发现并生成“小而难”的实例，有效暴露求解器的特定弱点。实验表明，EvolveGen能够高效生成多样化的基准测试集，格式符合标准（如AIGER和BTOR2），并能有效揭示当前先进模型检查器的性能瓶颈。

title: Transformers converge to invariant algorithmic cores
authors: Joshua S. Schiffman
abstract: Large language models exhibit sophisticated capabilities, yet understanding how they work internally remains a central challenge. A fundamental obstacle is that training selects for behavior, not circuitry, so many weight configurations can implement the same function. Which internal structures reflect the computation, and which are accidents of a particular training run? This work extracts algorithmic cores: compact subspaces necessary and sufficient for task performance. Independently trained transformers learn different weights but converge to the same cores. Markov-chain transformers embed 3D cores in nearly orthogonal subspaces yet recover identical transition spectra. Modular-addition transformers discover compact cyclic operators at grokking that later inflate, yielding a predictive model of the memorization-to-generalization transition. GPT-2 language models govern subject-verb agreement through a single axis that, when flipped, inverts grammatical number throughout generation across scales. These results reveal low-dimensional invariants that persist across training runs and scales, suggesting that transformer computations are organized around compact, shared algorithmic structures. Mechanistic interpretability could benefit from targeting such invariants -- the computational essence -- rather than implementation-specific details.
title_zh: 变压器收敛到不变的算法核心
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型展现出复杂而精妙的能力，但理解其内部运作机制仍是核心挑战。一个根本性障碍在于，训练过程选择的是行为表现，而非具体的电路结构，因此存在多种不同的权重配置都能实现相同功能。哪些内部结构真正反映了计算过程，哪些只是特定训练过程中的偶然产物？本研究提取出算法核心：能够充分且必要地支持任务执行的紧凑子空间。尽管独立训练的Transformer模型学习到不同的权重，却会收敛到相同的算法核心。马尔可夫链Transformer将三维核心嵌入近乎正交的子空间中，但仍能恢复完全相同的转移谱。模块化加法Transformer在“顿悟”阶段发现紧凑的循环算子，随后这些算子逐渐膨胀，从而构建出对记忆到泛化转变过程的预测模型。GPT-2语言模型通过单一轴线控制主谓一致，当该轴线被反转时，生成结果中的语法数特征在整个尺度上发生整体反转。这些结果揭示了在不同训练过程和模型规模下依然保持不变的低维不变量，表明Transformer的计算机制围绕着紧凑且共享的算法结构组织。机制可解释性研究或许应聚焦于这些不变量——即计算的本质——而非具体实现细节。

title: RAIN-Merging: A Gradient-Free Method to Enhance Instruction Following in Large Reasoning Models with Preserved Thinking Format
authors: Zhehao Huang, Yuhang Liu, Baijiong Lin, Yixin Lou, Zhengbao He, Hanling Tian, Tao Li, Xiaolin Huang
abstract: Large reasoning models (LRMs) excel at a long chain of reasoning but often fail to faithfully follow instructions regarding output format, constraints, or specific requirements. We investigate whether this gap can be closed by integrating an instruction-tuned model (ITM) into an LRM. Analyzing their differences in parameter space, namely task vectors, we find that their principal subspaces are nearly orthogonal across key modules, suggesting a lightweight merging with minimal interference. However, we also demonstrate that naive merges are fragile because they overlook the output format mismatch between LRMs (with explicit thinking and response segments) and ITMs (answers-only). We introduce RAIN-Merging (Reasoning-Aware Instruction-attention guided Null-space projection Merging), a gradient-free method that integrates instruction following while preserving thinking format and reasoning performance. First, with a small reasoning calibration set, we project the ITM task vector onto the null space of forward features at thinking special tokens, which preserves the LRM's structured reasoning mechanisms. Second, using a small instruction calibration set, we estimate instruction attention to derive module-specific scaling that amplifies instruction-relevant components and suppresses leakage. Across four instruction-following benchmarks and nine reasoning & general capability benchmarks, RAIN-Merging substantially improves instruction adherence while maintaining reasoning quality. The gains are consistent across model scales and architectures, translating to improved performance in agent settings.
title_zh: RAIN-Merging：一种无梯度方法，用于增强大型推理模型的指令遵循能力并保持思维格式
abstract_zh: 大型推理模型（LRMs）在长链推理方面表现出色，但常常无法忠实遵循关于输出格式、约束条件或特定要求的指令。我们探究了通过将指令微调模型（ITM）整合到LRM中，是否能够弥合这一差距。通过对两者在参数空间中的差异（即任务向量）进行分析，我们发现它们在关键模块的主子空间几乎正交，这表明可以实现轻量级融合且干扰极小。然而，我们也证明了简单的融合方法是脆弱的，因为它忽略了LRM（具有显式思考与回答两部分）与ITM（仅输出答案）之间在输出格式上的不匹配问题。为此，我们提出了RAIN-Merging（推理感知的指令注意力引导的零空间投影融合）方法，这是一种无需梯度的融合技术，能够在保持推理格式和推理性能的同时，增强指令遵循能力。首先，利用少量推理校准数据集，我们将ITM的任务向量投影到思考特殊标记的前向特征的零空间中，从而保留LRM的结构化推理机制。其次，通过少量指令校准数据集估计指令注意力，进而推导出模块级的缩放系数，以增强与指令相关的内容并抑制信息泄露。在四个指令遵循基准和九个推理与通用能力基准上，RAIN-Merging显著提升了指令遵循度，同时保持了高水平的推理质量。这种提升在不同模型规模和架构下均具有一致性，并在智能体应用场景中带来了性能的进一步优化。

title: Utilizing LLMs for Industrial Process Automation
authors: Salim Fares
abstract: A growing number of publications address the best practices to use Large Language Models (LLMs) for software engineering in recent years. However, most of this work focuses on widely-used general purpose programming languages like Python due to their widespread usage training data. The utility of LLMs for software within the industrial process automation domain, with highly-specialized languages that are typically only used in proprietary contexts, remains underexplored. This research aims to utilize and integrate LLMs in the industrial development process, solving real-life programming tasks (e.g., generating a movement routine for a robotic arm) and accelerating the development cycles of manufacturing systems.
title_zh: 利用大语言模型进行工业流程自动化
abstract_zh: 近年来，越来越多的出版物探讨了在软件工程中使用大型语言模型（LLMs）的最佳实践。然而，这些研究大多集中于Python等广泛使用的通用编程语言，因为这些语言拥有丰富的训练数据。相比之下，针对工业过程自动化领域中高度专业化、通常仅在专有环境中使用的编程语言，LLMs的应用潜力仍鲜有研究。本研究旨在将LLMs应用于工业开发流程，解决实际编程任务（例如，为机械臂生成运动程序），并加速制造系统的开发周期。

title: Real-Time Stream Compaction for Sparse Machine Learning on FPGAs
authors: Marc Neu, Isabel Haide, Torben Ferber, Jürgen Becker
abstract: Machine learning algorithms are being used more frequently in the first-level triggers in collider experiments, with Graph Neural Networks pushing the hardware requirements of FPGA-based triggers beyond the current state of the art. To meet the stringent demands of high-throughput and low-latency environments, we propose a concept for latency-optimized preprocessing of sparse sensor data, enabling efficient GNN hardware acceleration by removing dynamic input sparsity. Our approach rearranges data coming from a large number of First-In-First-Out interfaces, typically sensor frontends, to a smaller number of FIFO interfaces connected to a machine learning hardware accelerator. In order to achieve high throughput while minimizing the hardware utilization, we developed a hierarchical sparsity compression pipeline optimized for FPGAs. We implemented our concept in the Chisel design language as an open-source hardware generator. For demonstration, we implemented one configuration of our module as preprocessing stage in a GNN-based first-level trigger for the Electromagnetic Calorimeter inside the Belle II detector. Additionally we evaluate latency, throughput, resource utilization, and scalability for a wide range of parameters, to enable broader use for other large scale scientific experiments.
title_zh: 面向FPGA的稀疏机器学习实时流压缩
abstract_zh: 机器学习算法在对撞机实验的一级触发系统中正被越来越频繁地应用，其中图神经网络（GNN）已使基于FPGA的触发系统硬件需求超越了当前技术水平。为满足高吞吐量和低延迟环境的严苛要求，我们提出了一种面向延迟优化的稀疏传感器数据预处理概念，通过消除动态输入稀疏性，实现高效的GNN硬件加速。我们的方法将来自大量先进先出（FIFO）接口（通常为传感器前端）的数据重新组织为更少数量的FIFO接口，这些接口连接至机器学习硬件加速器。为在最小化硬件资源占用的同时实现高吞吐量，我们开发了一种专为FPGA优化的分层稀疏性压缩流水线。我们使用Chisel硬件设计语言实现了该概念，构建了一个开源硬件生成器。为验证其有效性，我们在Belle II探测器的电磁量能器中，将该模块的一个配置实现为基于GNN的一级触发系统的预处理阶段。此外，我们还针对多种参数范围评估了该方案的延迟、吞吐量、资源利用率及可扩展性，以支持其在其他大规模科学实验中的广泛应用。

title: Natural Language Declarative Prompting (NLD-P): A Modular Governance Method for Prompt Design Under Model Drift
authors: Hyunwoo Kim, Hanau Yi, Jaehee Bae, Yumin Kim
abstract: The rapid evolution of large language models (LLMs) has transformed prompt engineering from a localized craft into a systems-level governance challenge. As models scale and update across generations, prompt behavior becomes sensitive to shifts in instruction-following policies, alignment regimes, and decoding strategies, a phenomenon we characterize as GPT-scale model drift. Under such conditions, surface-level formatting conventions and ad hoc refinement are insufficient to ensure stable, interpretable control. This paper reconceptualizes Natural Language Declarative Prompting (NLD-P) as a declarative governance method rather than a rigid field template. NLD-P is formalized as a modular control abstraction that separates provenance, constraint logic, task content, and post-generation evaluation, encoded directly in natural language without reliance on external orchestration code. We define minimal compliance criteria, analyze model-dependent schema receptivity, and position NLD-P as an accessible governance framework for non-developer practitioners operating within evolving LLM ecosystems. Portions of drafting and editorial refinement employed a schema-bound LLM assistant configured under NLD-P. All conceptual framing, methodological claims, and final revisions were directed, reviewed, and approved by the human author under a documented human-in-the-loop protocol. The paper concludes by outlining implications for declarative control under ongoing model evolution and identifying directions for future empirical validation.
title_zh: 自然语言声明式提示（NLD-P）：一种应对模型漂移的提示设计模块化治理方法
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速演进，已将提示工程从一种局部性的技艺转变为系统级的治理挑战。随着模型在代际间不断扩展与更新，提示行为对指令遵循策略、对齐机制以及解码方法的微小变化变得极为敏感，这一现象我们称之为“GPT级模型漂移”。在这一背景下，仅依赖表面格式规范或临时性的优化手段已不足以保障控制的稳定性与可解释性。本文重新定义自然语言声明式提示（NLD-P）为一种声明式治理方法，而非僵化的字段模板。NLD-P被形式化为一种模块化的控制抽象，将来源信息、约束逻辑、任务内容与生成后评估分离，并直接以自然语言编码实现，无需依赖外部编排代码。我们提出了最小合规标准，分析了模型对不同模式的接受程度，并将NLD-P定位为面向非开发者实践者在不断演进的LLM生态中可访问的治理框架。论文起草与编辑优化过程中，部分工作借助了基于NLD-P配置的、具有模式约束的LLM助手完成。所有概念框架、方法论主张及最终修订均由人类作者在可追溯的“人在回路”协议下主导、审阅并批准。论文最后探讨了持续模型演进背景下声明式控制的深远影响，并指明了未来实证验证的研究方向。

title: CL4SE: A Context Learning Benchmark For Software Engineering Tasks
authors: Haichuan Hu, Ye Shang, Guoqing Xie, Congqing He, Quanjun Zhang
abstract: Context engineering has emerged as a pivotal paradigm for unlocking the potential of Large Language Models (LLMs) in Software Engineering (SE) tasks, enabling performance gains at test time without model fine-tuning. Despite its success, existing research lacks a systematic taxonomy of SE-specific context types and a dedicated benchmark to quantify the heterogeneous effects of different contexts across core SE workflows. To address this gap, we propose CL4SE (Context Learning for Software Engineering), a comprehensive benchmark featuring a fine-grained taxonomy of four SE-oriented context types (interpretable examples, project-specific context, procedural decision-making context, and positive & negative context), each mapped to a representative task (code generation, code summarization, code review, and patch correctness assessment). We construct high-quality datasets comprising over 13,000 samples from more than 30 open-source projects and evaluate five mainstream LLMs across nine metrics. Extensive experiments demonstrate that context learning yields an average performance improvement of 24.7% across all tasks. Specifically, procedural context boosts code review performance by up to 33% (Qwen3-Max), mixed positive-negative context improves patch assessment by 30% (DeepSeek-V3), project-specific context increases code summarization BLEU by 14.78% (GPT-Oss-120B), and interpretable examples enhance code generation PASS@1 by 5.72% (DeepSeek-V3). CL4SE establishes the first standardized evaluation framework for SE context learning, provides actionable empirical insights into task-specific context design, and releases a large-scale dataset to facilitate reproducible research in this domain.
title_zh: CL4SE：面向软件工程任务的上下文学习基准
abstract_zh: 上下文工程已发展成为释放大语言模型（LLMs）在软件工程（SE）任务中潜力的关键范式，能够在不进行模型微调的情况下，于测试阶段显著提升性能。尽管取得了成功，现有研究仍缺乏针对SE任务的上下文类型系统性分类，也缺少专门的基准来量化不同上下文在核心SE工作流中的异质性影响。为填补这一空白，我们提出了CL4SE（面向软件工程的上下文学习），这是一个全面的基准，包含四种面向SE的细粒度上下文类型（可解释示例、项目特定上下文、过程性决策上下文以及正负样本上下文），每种上下文类型均对应一个代表性任务（代码生成、代码摘要、代码审查和补丁正确性评估）。我们构建了高质量的数据集，涵盖来自30多个开源项目的超过13,000个样本，并在九项指标上对五种主流LLM进行了评估。大量实验表明，上下文学习在所有任务上平均提升了24.7%的性能。具体而言，过程性上下文使代码审查性能最高提升33%（Qwen3-Max），正负样本混合上下文使补丁评估性能提升30%（DeepSeek-V3），项目特定上下文使代码摘要的BLEU得分提升14.78%（GPT-Oss-120B），而可解释示例则使代码生成的PASS@1指标提升5.72%（DeepSeek-V3）。CL4SE建立了首个标准化的SE上下文学习评估框架，为特定任务的上下文设计提供了可操作的实证洞察，并公开发布了一个大规模数据集，以推动该领域的可复现研究。

title: Semantic Tube Prediction: Beating LLM Data Efficiency with JEPA
authors: Hai Huang, Yann LeCun, Randall Balestriero
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) obey consistent scaling laws -- empirical power-law fits that predict how loss decreases with compute, data, and parameters. While predictive, these laws are descriptive rather than prescriptive: they characterize typical training, not optimal training. Surprisingly few works have successfully challenged the data-efficiency bounds implied by these laws -- which is our primary focus. To that end, we introduce the Geodesic Hypothesis, positing that token sequences trace geodesics on a smooth semantic manifold and are therefore locally linear. Building on this principle, we propose a novel Semantic Tube Prediction (STP) task, a JEPA-style regularizer that confines hidden-state trajectories to a tubular neighborhood of the geodesic. STP generalizes JEPA to language without requiring explicit multi-view augmentations. We show this constraint improves signal-to-noise ratio, and consequently preserves diversity by preventing trajectory collisions during inference. Empirically, STP allows LLMs to match baseline accuracy with 16$\times$ less training data on the NL-RX-SYNTH dataset, directly violating the data term of Chinchilla-style scaling laws and demonstrating that principled geometric priors can surpass brute-force scaling. Code is available at https://github.com/galilai-group/llm-jepa#stp.
title_zh: 语义管预测：通过JEPA超越大语言模型的数据效率
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）遵循一致的缩放定律——这些经验性的幂律拟合能够预测损失如何随计算量、数据量和参数量的变化而减少。尽管这些定律具有预测能力，但它们本质上是描述性的而非指导性的：它们刻画了典型的训练过程，而非最优训练过程。令人惊讶的是，极少有研究成功挑战了这些定律所隐含的数据效率上限——这正是我们研究的重点。为此，我们提出了“测地线假设”（Geodesic Hypothesis），即认为词元序列在平滑的语义流形上沿测地线演化，因此在局部上呈现线性特征。基于这一原理，我们提出了一种新颖的语义管预测（Semantic Tube Prediction, STP）任务，这是一种类似JEPA的正则化方法，它将隐藏状态轨迹限制在测地线的管状邻域内。STP将JEPA推广至语言模型领域，且无需依赖显式的多视图增强。我们证明，该约束能够提升信噪比，从而在推理过程中通过防止轨迹碰撞来保持多样性。实证结果表明，在NL-RX-SYNTH数据集上，STP使LLM在仅使用基线1/16的训练数据情况下即可达到相当的准确率，直接违背了Chinchilla式缩放定律中的数据项，证明了基于几何先验的合理设计可以超越单纯的规模扩展。代码已开源：https://github.com/galilai-group/llm-jepa#stp。

title: ESAA: Event Sourcing for Autonomous Agents in LLM-Based Software Engineering
authors: Elzo Brito dos Santos Filho
abstract: Autonomous agents based on Large Language Models (LLMs) have evolved from reactive assistants to systems capable of planning, executing actions via tools, and iterating over environment observations. However, they remain vulnerable to structural limitations: lack of native state, context degradation over long horizons, and the gap between probabilistic generation and deterministic execution requirements. This paper presents the ESAA (Event Sourcing for Autonomous Agents) architecture, which separates the agent's cognitive intention from the project's state mutation, inspired by the Event Sourcing pattern. In ESAA, agents emit only structured intentions in validated JSON (agent.result or issue.report); a deterministic orchestrator validates, persists events in an append-only log (activity.jsonl), applies file-writing effects, and projects a verifiable materialized view (roadmap.json). The proposal incorporates boundary contracts (AGENT_CONTRACT.yaml), metaprompting profiles (PARCER), and replay verification with hashing (esaa verify), ensuring the immutability of completed tasks and forensic traceability. Two case studies validate the architecture: (i) a landing page project (9 tasks, 49 events, single-agent composition) and (ii) a clinical dashboard system (50 tasks, 86 events, 4 concurrent agents across 8 phases), both concluding with run.status=success and verify_status=ok. The multi-agent case study demonstrates real concurrent orchestration with heterogeneous LLMs (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Codex GPT-5, Antigravity/Gemini 3 Pro, and Claude Opus 4.6), providing empirical evidence of the architecture's scalability beyond single-agent scenarios.
title_zh: ESAA：基于大语言模型软件工程的自主代理事件溯源
abstract_zh: 基于大语言模型（LLM）的自主智能体已从被动响应的助手演进为具备规划能力、通过工具执行动作并根据环境观测进行迭代的系统。然而，这类智能体仍面临结构性局限：缺乏原生状态管理、长期任务中上下文信息逐渐退化，以及概率性生成与确定性执行需求之间的鸿沟。本文提出ESAA（Event Sourcing for Autonomous Agents）架构，该架构受事件溯源（Event Sourcing）模式启发，将智能体的认知意图与项目状态的变更相分离。在ESAA中，智能体仅以经过验证的JSON格式输出结构化意图（如agent.result或issue.report）；一个确定性编排器负责验证这些意图，将其持久化至追加-only日志（activity.jsonl），执行文件写入等副作用操作，并生成可验证的物化视图（roadmap.json）。该方案引入边界契约（AGENT_CONTRACT.yaml）、元提示配置文件（PARCER）以及基于哈希的回放验证机制（esaa verify），确保已完成任务的不可篡改性与可追溯的审计能力。两个案例研究验证了该架构的有效性：（i）一个落地页项目（9个任务，49个事件，单智能体组合）；（ii）一个临床仪表盘系统（50个任务，86个事件，4个并发智能体跨越8个阶段），两者均成功完成，运行状态为run.status=success，验证状态为verify_status=ok。多智能体案例研究展示了异构LLM（Claude Sonnet 4.6、Codex GPT-5、Antigravity/Gemini 3 Pro、Claude Opus 4.6）在真实并发场景下的协同编排能力，为该架构在单智能体之外的可扩展性提供了实证支持。

title: Automated Vulnerability Detection in Source Code Using Deep Representation Learning
authors: C. Seas, G. Fitzpatrick, J. A. Hamilton, M. C. Carlisle
abstract: Each year, software vulnerabilities are discovered, which pose significant risks of exploitation and system compromise. We present a convolutional neural network model that can successfully identify bugs in C code. We trained our model using two complementary datasets: a machine-labeled dataset created by Draper Labs using three static analyzers and the NIST SATE Juliet human-labeled dataset designed for testing static analyzers. In contrast with the work of Russell et al. on these datasets, we focus on C programs, enabling us to specialize and optimize our detection techniques for this language. After removing duplicates from the dataset, we tokenize the input into 91 token categories. The category values are converted to a binary vector to save memory. Our first convolution layer is chosen so that the entire encoding of the token is presented to the filter. We use two convolution and pooling layers followed by two fully connected layers to classify programs into either a common weakness enumeration category or as ``clean.'' We obtain higher recall than prior work by Russell et al. on this dataset when requiring high precision. We also demonstrate on a custom Linux kernel dataset that we are able to find real vulnerabilities in complex code with a low false-positive rate.
title_zh: 基于深度表示学习的源代码自动漏洞检测
abstract_zh: 每年都会发现软件漏洞，这些漏洞存在被利用和导致系统被攻破的重大风险。本文提出了一种卷积神经网络模型，能够有效识别C语言代码中的缺陷。我们使用两个互补的数据集对模型进行训练：由Draper实验室利用三种静态分析工具生成的机器标注数据集，以及NIST SATE Juliet人工标注数据集，该数据集专为测试静态分析工具而设计。与Russell等人在这些数据集上的研究不同，我们专注于C语言程序，从而能够针对该语言专门化并优化我们的检测技术。在去除数据集中的重复项后，我们将输入文本分词为91个标记类别，类别值被转换为二进制向量以节省内存。我们的第一层卷积层设计使得整个标记编码信息都能完整地传递给卷积核。随后，我们采用两层卷积与池化层，再接两层全连接层，将程序分类为“常见弱性枚举”类别或“无缺陷”类别。在保持高精度的前提下，我们的模型在该数据集上的召回率高于Russell等人先前的工作。此外，我们在一个自定义的Linux内核数据集上进行了验证，结果表明，我们的方法能够在复杂代码中发现真实存在的漏洞，同时保持较低的误报率。

title: Towards Better RL Training Data Utilization via Second-Order Rollout
authors: Zhe Yang, Yudong Wang, Rang Li, Zhifang Sui
abstract: Reinforcement Learning (RL) has empowered Large Language Models (LLMs) with strong reasoning capabilities, but vanilla RL mainly focuses on generation capability improvement by training with only first-order rollout (generating multiple responses for a question), and we argue that this approach fails to fully exploit the potential of training data because of the neglect of critique capability training. To tackle this problem, we further introduce the concept of second-order rollout (generating multiple critiques for a response) and propose a unified framework for jointly training generation and critique capabilities. Extensive experiments across various models and datasets demonstrate that our approach can utilize training data more effectively than vanilla RL and achieve better performance under the same training data. Additionally, we uncover several insightful findings regarding second-order rollout and critique training, such as the importance of label balance in critique training and the noise problem of outcome-based rewards, which can be mitigated through sampling techniques. Our work offers a preliminary exploration of dynamic data augmentation and joint generation-critique training in RL, providing meaningful inspiration for the further advancement of RL training
title_zh: 通过二阶轨迹提升强化学习训练数据利用效率
abstract_zh: 强化学习（RL）已赋予大型语言模型（LLMs）强大的推理能力，但传统的强化学习主要通过仅使用一阶回溯（为一个问题生成多个回答）来训练，从而提升生成能力，我们认为这种方法因忽视了批判能力的训练，未能充分挖掘训练数据的潜力。为解决这一问题，我们进一步引入了二阶回溯（为一个回答生成多个批判意见）的概念，并提出了一种统一框架，用于联合训练生成与批判能力。在多种模型和数据集上的大量实验表明，与传统强化学习相比，我们的方法能更有效地利用训练数据，并在相同训练数据条件下实现更优的性能。此外，我们还揭示了关于二阶回溯和批判训练的若干深刻发现，例如批判训练中标签平衡的重要性，以及基于结果的奖励所存在的噪声问题，这些问题可通过采样技术得到缓解。我们的工作初步探索了强化学习中的动态数据增强与生成-批判联合训练，为强化学习训练的进一步发展提供了有意义的启发。

title: Search-P1: Path-Centric Reward Shaping for Stable and Efficient Agentic RAG Training
authors: Tianle Xia, Ming Xu, Lingxiang Hu, Yiding Sun, Wenwei Li, Linfang Shang, Liqun Liu, Peng Shu, Huan Yu, Jie Jiang
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) by incorporating external knowledge, yet traditional single-round retrieval struggles with complex multi-step reasoning. Agentic RAG addresses this by enabling LLMs to dynamically decide when and what to retrieve, but current RL-based training methods suffer from sparse outcome rewards that discard intermediate signals and low sample efficiency where failed samples contribute nothing. We propose Search-P1, a framework that introduces path-centric reward shaping for agentic RAG training, comprising two key components: (1) Path-Centric Reward, which evaluates the structural quality of reasoning trajectories through order-agnostic step coverage and soft scoring that extracts learning signals even from failed samples, and (2) Dual-Track Path Scoring with offline-generated reference planners that assesses paths from both self-consistency and reference-alignment perspectives. Experiments on multiple QA benchmarks demonstrate that Search-P1 achieves significant improvements over Search-R1 and other strong baselines, with an average accuracy gain of 7.7 points.
title_zh: 搜索-P1：基于路径的奖励塑造用于稳定高效的代理式RAG训练
abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）通过引入外部知识来提升大型语言模型（LLM）的能力，但传统的单轮检索在处理复杂多步推理任务时存在局限。代理式RAG（Agentic RAG）通过使LLM能够动态决定何时以及检索什么信息，有效缓解了这一问题。然而，当前基于强化学习（RL）的训练方法面临奖励稀疏的问题，即中间过程的信号被忽略，且样本效率低下，失败样本无法提供有效学习信息。为此，我们提出Search-P1框架，通过路径中心化的奖励设计来优化代理式RAG的训练，包含两个核心组件：（1）路径中心化奖励（Path-Centric Reward），通过不依赖顺序的步骤覆盖率和软评分机制，评估推理轨迹的结构质量，即使在失败样本中也能提取有效的学习信号；（2）双轨路径评分机制，结合离线生成的参考规划器，从自洽性和与参考路径的一致性两个角度对路径进行评估。在多个问答基准上的实验表明，Search-P1显著优于Search-R1及其他强基线模型，平均准确率提升达7.7个百分点。

title: ClawMobile: Rethinking Smartphone-Native Agentic Systems
authors: Hongchao Du, Shangyu Wu, Qiao Li, Riwei Pan, Jinheng Li, Youcheng Sun, Chun Jason Xue
abstract: Smartphones represent a uniquely challenging environment for agentic systems. Unlike cloud or desktop settings, mobile devices combine constrained execution contexts, fragmented control interfaces, and rapidly changing application states. As large language models (LLMs) evolve from conversational assistants to action-oriented agents, achieving reliable smartphone-native autonomy requires rethinking how reasoning and control are composed. We introduce ClawMobile as a concrete exploration of this design space. ClawMobile adopts a hierarchical architecture that separates high-level language reasoning from structured, deterministic control pathways, improving execution stability and reproducibility on real devices. Using ClawMobile as a case study, we distill the design principles for mobile LLM runtimes and identify key challenges in efficiency, adaptability, and stability. We argue that building robust smartphone-native agentic systems demands principled coordination between probabilistic planning and deterministic system interfaces. The implementation is open-sourced~\footnote{https://github.com/ClawMobile/ClawMobile} to facilitate future exploration.
title_zh: 爪子移动：重新思考原生智能手机智能体系统
abstract_zh: 智能手机为智能体系统提供了一个独特而具有挑战性的环境。与云端或桌面环境不同，移动设备具有受限的执行上下文、碎片化的控制界面以及快速变化的应用状态。随着大型语言模型（LLMs）从对话式助手演变为以行动为导向的智能体，实现真正可靠的原生智能手机自主性，需要重新思考推理与控制的组合方式。为此，我们提出了ClawMobile，作为这一设计空间的具体探索。ClawMobile采用分层架构，将高层语言推理与结构化、确定性的控制路径相分离，从而在真实设备上提升了执行的稳定性与可复现性。通过ClawMobile这一案例研究，我们提炼出移动设备上LLM运行时的设计原则，并识别出效率、适应性与稳定性方面的关键挑战。我们认为，构建稳健的原生智能手机智能体系统，需要在概率性规划与确定性系统接口之间实现有原则的协同。该系统的实现已开源~\footnote{https://github.com/ClawMobile/ClawMobile}，以促进未来的研究探索。

title: Knowledge-Driven Multi-Agent AI for Interpretable Yield Optimization in Semiconductor Manufacturing
authors: Chaojun Geng, Degang Wang, Yanan Liu, Xiupeng Shi
publish: 2025 4th International Conference on Artificial Intelligence, Human-Computer Interaction and Robotics (AIHCIR)
abstract: Optimizing yield in semiconductor manufacturing is economically critical but challenging because process data are kept highly confidential and anonymized. Traditional machine-learning methods rely on statistical correlations and cannot incorporate physical process knowledge, limiting model interpretability and transferability. We propose a multi-agent AutoML framework that uses large language and vision models to extract physics-based formulas from the literature and inject them into anonymized process data for feature construction. Five autonomous agents retrieve relevant papers, parse formulas, cluster the data, align abstract formula variables with data features, and construct interpretable formula-driven features. On the SECOM dataset, this knowledge-driven approach expands the feature set by 37.6% and improves AUC from 79.85% to 90.59%, with 81.8% of the top predictive features derived from literature formulas. A copula-based recommender then simulates process adjustments by shifting low-yield samples toward high-yield feature regions, raising the mean predicted yield probability from 0.15 to 0.76. Our framework demonstrates how cross-modal knowledge integration and multi-agent optimization can deliver transparent, transferable yield-improvement strategies under strict data confidentiality.
title_zh: 基于知识的多智能体人工智能在半导体制造可解释性良率优化中的应用
abstract_zh: 在半导体制造中优化良率具有重要的经济意义，但面临巨大挑战，因为工艺数据通常高度保密且经过匿名化处理。传统的机器学习方法依赖于统计相关性，无法融入物理工艺知识，从而限制了模型的可解释性和可迁移性。我们提出了一种多智能体自动化机器学习（AutoML）框架，利用大语言模型和视觉模型从文献中提取基于物理规律的公式，并将其注入匿名化的工艺数据中，用于特征构建。五个自主智能体分别负责：检索相关论文、解析公式、聚类数据、将抽象公式中的变量与数据特征对齐，以及构建可解释的、由公式驱动的特征。在SECOM数据集上的实验表明，该知识驱动方法使特征集扩大了37.6%，AUC从79.85%提升至90.59%，其中81.8%的顶级预测特征源自文献中的公式。随后，基于Copula的推荐系统通过将低良率样本向高良率特征区域迁移，模拟工艺调整，使预测良率的平均概率从0.15提升至0.76。本框架展示了跨模态知识融合与多智能体优化如何在严格数据保密条件下，实现透明、可迁移的良率提升策略。

title: Can an LLM Induce a Graph? Investigating Memory Drift and Context Length
authors: Raquib Bin Yousuf, Aadyant Khatri, Shengzhe Xu, Mandar Sharma, Naren Ramakrishnan
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Knowledge Graph (ICKG)
abstract: Recently proposed evaluation benchmarks aim to characterize the effective context length and the forgetting tendencies of large language models (LLMs). However, these benchmarks often rely on simplistic “needle in a haystack” retrieval or continuation tasks that may not accurately reflect the performance of these models in information-dense scenarios. Thus, rather than simple next token prediction, we argue for evaluating these models on more complex reasoning tasks that requires them to induce structured relational knowledge from the text - such as graphs from potentially noisy natural language content. While the input text can be viewed as generated in terms of a graph, its structure is not made explicit and connections must be induced from distributed textual cues, separated by long contexts and interspersed with irrelevant information. Our findings reveal that LLMs begin to exhibit memory drift and contextual forgetting at much shorter effective lengths when tasked with this form of relational reasoning, compared to what existing benchmarks suggest. With these findings, we offer recommendations for the optimal use of popular LLMs for complex reasoning tasks. We further show that even models specialized for reasoning, such as OpenAI o1, remain vulnerable to early memory drift in these settings. These results point to significant limitations in the models' ability to abstract structured knowledge from unstructured input and highlight the need for architectural adaptations to improve long-range reasoning. Our codebase to support reproducibility is publicly available.<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup><sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1</sup>https://github.com/DiscoveryAnalyticsCenter/MemoryDrift.
title_zh: 大语言模型能否生成图结构？探究记忆漂移与上下文长度的影响
abstract_zh:
最近提出的评估基准旨在刻画大型语言模型（LLMs）的有效上下文长度及其遗忘倾向。然而，这些基准通常依赖于简单的“在稻草堆中找针”式的检索或续写任务，这类任务可能无法准确反映模型在信息密集场景下的实际表现。因此，我们主张不应仅基于简单的下一个词预测来评估模型，而应将其置于更复杂的推理任务中，要求模型从文本中推导出结构化的关系知识——例如从可能存在噪声的自然语言内容中构建图结构。尽管输入文本在某种意义上可被视为由图生成，但其结构并未显式表达，模型必须从分散的文本线索中推断出关联关系，而这些线索往往被长距离的上下文隔开，并夹杂着无关信息。

我们的研究发现，当模型被要求进行这种关系推理时，其记忆漂移和上下文遗忘现象在远短于现有基准所暗示的有效长度下便已显现。基于这些发现，我们提出了针对主流大型语言模型在复杂推理任务中最佳使用方式的建议。此外，我们还发现，即便是专为推理设计的模型（如OpenAI o1），在这些场景下依然容易出现早期记忆漂移。这些结果揭示了当前模型从非结构化输入中抽象出结构化知识能力的显著局限性，也凸显了亟需通过架构改进来提升长程推理能力。为支持研究的可复现性，我们的代码库已公开发布。[1] https://github.com/DiscoveryAnalyticsCenter/MemoryDrift

title: Accelerating the A2D ocean model with Standard Language Parallelism
authors: Bingrui Chen, Yizhong Chen, Hongyuan Guo, Jianrong Zhu
abstract: Parallel computing is crucial for enhancing the computational efficiency of ocean numerical models. Traditionally, parallelization in such models relied primarily on MPI. Subsequent developments introduced GPU-oriented computing tools, including CUDA and OpenACC, which enable accelerated computation on NVIDIA GPUs. However, each tool has its own syntax and lacks interoperability. Recent support for Standard Language Parallelism from major Fortran compiler vendors (e.g., Intel, NVIDIA) has unlocked efficient parallelization across multi-core CPUs and GPUs from a single Fortran codebase. This study employs Standard Language Parallelism to accelerate the A2D model, a two-dimensional unstructured-grid ocean numerical model, by parallelizing loops in its source code. The refactored code can be compiled for either multi-core CPU or GPU execution simply by selecting appropriate compiling flags. This approach offers advantages including high code readability, broad hardware compatibility, and reduced maintenance overhead. Using compilers such as ifort or nvfortran, the parallelized model achieves speedups exceeding 23× in both multi-core and GPU configurations compared to the original serial implementation.
title_zh: 使用标准语言并行加速A2D海洋模型
abstract_zh: 并行计算对于提升海洋数值模型的计算效率至关重要。传统上，这类模型的并行化主要依赖于MPI。随后，出现了面向GPU的计算工具，如CUDA和OpenACC，使得在NVIDIA GPU上实现加速计算成为可能。然而，每种工具都有其独特的语法，且彼此之间缺乏互操作性。近年来，主流Fortran编译器厂商（如Intel、NVIDIA）对标准语言并行性的支持，使得仅通过单一Fortran代码库即可高效地在多核CPU和GPU上实现并行化。本研究采用标准语言并行性技术，对一个二维非结构化网格海洋数值模型A2D进行了加速，通过并行化其源代码中的循环实现性能提升。重构后的代码仅需选择相应的编译标志，即可分别在多核CPU或GPU上进行编译和执行。该方法具有代码可读性高、硬件兼容性广以及维护成本低等优势。使用ifort或nvfortran等编译器，与原始串行实现相比，该并行化模型在多核CPU和GPU配置下均实现了超过23倍的加速比。
